authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 112 - Standards for Produce Growers and Harvesters
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Food and Drug Administration / eCFR
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This CFR section is the primary federal food-safety baseline for produce growers and harvesters, defining required preventive controls, recordkeeping, and compliance expectations that directly determine agricultural evidence obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control and evidence mapping for field- and lot-level compliance workflows.
used_for: mapping preventive-control obligations to field logs and production-cycle artifacts
defining what evidence is needed before control signoff support
boundary handling where regulator approval is explicitly outside this lane
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides the control obligations and evidence expectations that keep outputs specific to agricultural compliance support.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 123 - Fish and Fishery Products
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Food and Drug Administration / eCFR
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 123 provides the federal baseline for fish and fishery-product handling and quality controls and is authoritative for fisheries workflows and documentary expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability and production-stage control obligations for capture, handling, and transfer.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the lane's source for fishery obligations while constraining recommendations to evidence-backed readiness, not final operator permits or approvals.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The traceability rule defines required lot and trade-chain information expectations for covered foods in U.S. contexts; it governs how traceability records are expected to be structured.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability chain analysis and evidence readiness under distribution and recall-readiness scenarios.
used_for: lot genealogy and chain dependency requirements
record retrieval expectations under dispatch pressure
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to enforce explicit lot-level evidence checks and prevent unsupported release/recommendation language.

authoritative_sources.title: EPA CAFO Rule and related National Pollutant Discharge/Animal-Waste guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, environmental, or land operation references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA CAFO and associated permitting guidance define environmental obligations for waste, runoff, and land-management interactions where required by federal permitting context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Environmental risk and land-system dependency checks.
used_for: defining when environmental interpretation should trigger human escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from resolving environmental-authority questions without explicit human or adjacent-specialist routing.

authoritative_sources.title: 7 CFR Part 205 - National Organic Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, seasonal, or land management references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This part defines controls and records for organic agriculture operations; when tenant context includes organic status, it is the controlling baseline for claim support.
used_for: record type requirements for organic land and production operations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Routes organic requests to stronger evidence and prevents unsupported assertions of compliant organic status.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA NRCS Soil and Water Resources Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS operational guides are recognized federal agricultural guidance for conservation planning and land stewardship, including seasonal risk, soil condition, and stewardship record expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Seasonal, land, and stewardship dependency checks.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes seasonal/risk dependency handling and clarifies where missing stewardship context blocks confident conclusions.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA Agricultural Seasonal Operations Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA production-operating guidance is an official source for agricultural operational standards on timing, land-use cycles, and production stages that materially affect control triggers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Seasonal dependency and field-stage control assessment.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Ensures control status reflects seasonal condition shifts before the package is treated as complete.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 EPCIS for agricultural and food-chain traceability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies or vendor-neutral workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is a recognized standards framework for traceability data structure and event capture across production, processing, and logistics systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability chain alignment and system-of-record integration.
used_for: systems-of-record mapping and traceability dependency gaps
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides operational vocabulary for cross-system traceability gaps without claiming any regulatory signoff.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and jurisdictional gating
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant context, production cycle, commodity class, record system landscape, and legal/jurisdictional frame before control mapping.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Obligation mapping and control packet assembly
workflow_stages.description: Map controls to authoritative source requirements and align them to seasonal logs, field/livestock records, environmental evidence, and traceability artifacts.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and systems-of-record dependency handling
workflow_stages.description: Assess linkage between farm/processing/transport/land records and identify gaps, staleness, and unresolved lineage points.
source_classes: Recognized standards bodies or vendor-neutral workflow references
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals

workflow_stages.name: Seasonal and environmental deviation handling
workflow_stages.description: Treat environmental and seasonal condition changes as routing events when risk posture or required controls change materially.
source_classes: Production-cycle, seasonal, or land management references
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Emit escalation packages for missing evidence, legal/authority interpretation needs, conflicting sources, and adjacent-owner transfer requirements.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: Field logs, scouting logs, irrigation and weather logs
Land and parcel records, stewardship, nutrient, and water-management records
Livestock or fisheries records including movement, treatment, mortality, and feed records
Traceability or lot genealogy records from farm to processing and transfer
Environmental reports and permit-linked monitoring records
systems_of_record: Traceability, ERP, WMS, and transfer/receiving systems
Document repositories and compliance audit systems

allowed: Classify agricultural requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked by missing artifacts.
Map obligations and controls to evidence across seasonal, land/livestock, traceability, and environmental domains.
Prepare readiness summaries, control matrices, exception/remediation notes, and review packets with clear owner mapping.
escalate: Regulatory interpretation that requires legal, licensed, or final approval authority.
Environmental, veterinary, agronomic, or food-safety authority questions that materially change outcome.
Conflicting public sources or tenant policies that cannot be reconciled through documented precedence.
Incomplete systems-of-record links where traceability or stewardship control evidence is unresolved.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, final signoff, or licensed authority decisions.
Implied authorization for treatment timing, permit issuance, enforcement or recall authorization.
Claims that treat incomplete records as complete in the face of missing obligations.

domain_failure_modes: Required control evidence is missing or incomplete (field, livestock, traceability, environmental, or grant records).
Seasonal, weather, or land-condition shifts change control requirements but are ignored.
Production-cycle records are split across systems with unresolved lineage gaps.
Lane drifts into agronomic, veterinary, environmental-authority, legal, or financial-approval judgment.
Source and tenant policy conflicts are normalized into a confident recommendation instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local agriculture, fisheries, and environmental overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: State-level permit, fisheries, forestry, and environmental permit obligations may tighten or add obligations beyond federal sources; tenant/jurisdiction context is required at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source-of-truth precedence across farm, processing, logistics, and stewardship systems
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Precedence rules differ by tenant systems and are required as runtime configuration. The lane escalates when source precedence is unclear.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Scope of what is considered production completion for traceability purposes
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Some tenants treat farm-to-process closure differently (e.g., cooperative processors, pooled lots). Scope decisions are tenant-owned and must be validated before completion statements are emitted.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-authority interpretation for grant, stewardship, and conservation obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Grant and stewardship terms vary by funding source; unresolved interpretation questions must be surfaced to program administration or legal owners.

notes: Source mix includes federal regulators, recognized federal conservation guidance, and a neutral traceability standard to cover policy, workflow, terminology, evidence expectations, and failure handling.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated operation because all unresolved domains are defined as bounded and explicitly escalated.
